question: Are all pedestrians currently walking on the crosswalks (including the central intersection and all side crossings) completely removed?
Are the shadows cast by the removed pedestrians fully eliminated from the road surface?
Is the edited surface free of any partial human fragments (e.g., isolated feet, luggage) or ghost silhouettes?
Do the restored crosswalk stripes maintain correct geometric perspective, alignment, and continuity where pedestrians were removed?
Is the restored asphalt texture consistent with the surrounding road, avoiding smooth smears, repeated patterns, or blurry patches?
Are the pedestrians standing on the sidewalks and corners (outside the crosswalk zones) preserved without alteration?
Are all vehicles (taxis, cars, vans) preserved exactly as they were in the original image?
Are the surrounding buildings, billboards, and digital advertisements preserved without distortion or text warping?
Are lane markings, arrows, and stop lines outside the removal zones preserved unchanged?
Is the edited area free of newly hallucinated objects (e.g., traffic cones, random debris) replacing the pedestrians?
Is the edited image free from visible seams, edge halos, or inconsistent lighting in the inpainted regions?
Does the edited image maintain consistent sharpness and resolution across the intersection compared to the unedited areas?
